sources: The No-Till Organic Vegetable Farm — Daniel Mays
The No-Till Growers Handbook — Jesse Frost

crops.name: Radish
crops.notes: Succession every 2 weeks spring/fall.
crops.frost_note: Fast fall/spring crop; handles light frost.

crops.name: Spinach
crops.notes: Best spring/fall. Bolt-resistant in cool weather.

crops.name: Lettuce
crops.notes: Transplant 4 rows on 30in bed. Cut-and-come varieties possible.
crops.frost_note: Light frost tolerant; cold frame extends into winter.

crops.name: Arugula
crops.notes: Fastest cash crop. Bolts in heat. Succession every 2 wks.
crops.frost_note: Handles light frost; protected tunnel extends to winter.

crops.name: Swiss Chard
crops.notes: Cut outer leaves — plant continues producing 60+ days.
crops.frost_note: Survives light frost; row cover extends harvest into winter.

crops.name: Mizuna
crops.notes: Excellent baby greens mix component. Very fast.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 25°F; excellent for late-season harvest.

crops.name: Tatsoi
crops.notes: Spoon-shaped leaves; excellent flavour in cool temps.
crops.frost_note: Survives to 22°F; flavor improves after frost.

crops.name: Mustard Greens
crops.notes: Spicy flavour; popular in salad mix. Heat-tolerant.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 28°F; good for fall extension.

crops.name: Mâche
crops.notes: Cold-hardy; excellent overwintering crop.
crops.frost_note: Classic winter salad green — survives well below 20°F.

crops.name: Sorrel
crops.notes: Perennial; lemony flavour; cut-and-come-again.
crops.frost_note: Perennial; dies back and re-sprouts in spring.

crops.name: Radicchio
crops.notes: Fall crop — blanch for 3 weeks before harvest. Bitter, premium market.
crops.frost_note: Improves in flavor after frost; harvest fall through winter.

crops.name: Endive/Escarole
crops.notes: Gourmet salad green; blanch heads for mild flavour.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 28°F; use as fall/winter crop.

crops.name: Asian Greens Mix
crops.notes: Fast cut-and-come mix; excellent for CSA.

crops.name: Watercress
crops.notes: Aquatic/moist soil preference. Very high value.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to light frost; protect crown from hard freeze.

crops.name: Purslane
crops.notes: Omega-3 rich; heat-tolerant summer green.

crops.name: Kale
crops.notes: Heavy feeder. MUST follow legume or cover crop.
crops.frost_note: Survives to 10°F; sweetens after frost. True overwinter crop.

crops.name: Broccoli
crops.notes: Early spring or fall. Side-shoots extend harvest.
crops.frost_note: Handles to 25°F; Purple Sprouting types can overwinter.

crops.name: Cabbage
crops.notes: Harvest in single flush. Follow with cover crop.

crops.name: Pac Choi
crops.notes: Spring and fall. Bolts in summer.

crops.name: Kohlrabi
crops.notes: Fast and prolific. Harvest at golf-ball size.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 25°F; harvest before hard freeze.

crops.name: Cauliflower
crops.notes: Blanch heads for white curds. Needs consistent moisture.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 28°F; self-blanching types for fall.

crops.name: Brussels Sprouts
crops.notes: Long season; plant in spring for fall harvest.
crops.frost_note: Sweetens after hard frost; harvest into December.

crops.name: Collards
crops.notes: Southern staple; very cold hardy. Cut-and-come-again.
crops.frost_note: Survives to 15°F; traditional Southern winter green.

crops.name: Napa Cabbage
crops.notes: Fall specialty. Bolts in heat; best for cool seasons.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 25°F; good for late fall.

crops.name: Romanesco
crops.notes: Premium market variety; fractal heads attract attention.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 28°F; plant for fall harvest.

crops.name: Carrots
crops.notes: Needs loose soil >12in deep. Pelleted seed preferred.
crops.frost_note: Survives in ground to 20°F; sweetens after frost.

crops.name: Beets
crops.notes: Multi-germ seed — thin to 4in. Greens marketable too.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 25°F; mulch to extend in-ground storage.

crops.name: Turnips
crops.notes: Hakurei is premium market variety. Harvest small for salad.

crops.name: Parsnip
crops.notes: Long season root; sweetens after frost. Direct seed early.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 0°F in ground. Leave all winter; dig in spring.

crops.name: Celeriac
crops.notes: Needs 4 months. Start early, transplant after hardening.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 25°F; mulch heavily for in-ground storage.

crops.name: Daikon
crops.notes: Excellent cover crop companion. Fall specialty.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 28°F; harvest before hard freeze.

crops.name: Rutabaga
crops.notes: Storage root; plant spring for fall harvest.

crops.name: Potatoes
crops.notes: Plant seed potatoes 12in apart. Hill twice. Storage crop.

crops.name: Sweet Potato
crops.notes: Needs long warm season. Plant slips after last frost.

crops.name: Sunchoke
crops.notes: Perennial; invasive — contain to dedicated bed. Nutty flavour.
crops.frost_note: Perennial; tubers survive freezing in ground.

crops.name: Hamburg Parsley
crops.notes: Dual-use: roots and leaves both harvested.

crops.name: Salsify
crops.notes: Oyster-flavoured root. Niche premium market.
crops.frost_note: Leave in ground all winter; improved flavor after frost.

crops.name: Scallions
crops.notes: Succession every 3 wks. Bundle 8-10/bunch.

crops.name: Leeks
crops.notes: Long season; transplant in furrows and hill up. Premium market.
crops.frost_note: Overwinters in zones 5+; harvest through winter.

crops.name: Storage Onion
crops.notes: Start early; long day types for northern zones.
crops.frost_note: Storage onions cure and keep; overwintering sets possible.

crops.name: Cipollini Onion
crops.notes: Premium market onion; flat disk shape. Stores well.

crops.name: Garlic
crops.notes: Plant cloves in fall; harvest following summer. Elite flavour.
crops.frost_note: Plant in fall, overwinters, harvest next summer.

crops.name: Shallots
crops.notes: Sets multiply; excellent CSA and farmers market item.

crops.name: Chives
crops.notes: Perennial herb. Cut-and-come-again all season.
crops.frost_note: Perennial; dies back and re-sprouts reliably each spring.

crops.name: Ramps
crops.notes: Perennial woodland crop; takes 3 years to produce. Very high value.

crops.name: Peas
crops.notes: Nitrogen fixer. Trellis required.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 28°F; best in early spring or fall.

crops.name: Green Beans
crops.notes: Succession plant every 3 weeks. Pick every 3-4 days.

crops.name: Edamame
crops.notes: Harvest when pods are plump but still green.

crops.name: Fava Beans
crops.notes: Cool-season only; plant early spring or fall.
crops.frost_note: Overwinters in zones 6+; plant fall for spring harvest.

crops.name: Cowpeas
crops.notes: Heat-tolerant nitrogen fixer; dual use as cover crop.

crops.name: Runner Beans
crops.notes: Vertical grower; ornamental flowers attract pollinators.

crops.name: Snap Peas
crops.notes: Dwarf habit; less trellis than standard snap peas.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 28°F; spring or fall planting.

crops.name: Soybeans
crops.notes: Dried or edamame-fresh. Warm-season nitrogen fixer.

crops.name: Cilantro
crops.notes: Succession every 3 wks. Crush seed before planting.

crops.name: Basil
crops.notes: Frost kills immediately. Transplant only after soil >60°F.

crops.name: Parsley
crops.notes: Slow germination — soak 24h. Biennial.

crops.name: Dill
crops.notes: Bolts in heat. Succession 3-4 wk intervals.

crops.name: Thyme
crops.notes: Perennial; drought-tolerant. Cut-and-come-again.
crops.frost_note: Perennial shrub; very cold hardy to -10°F.

crops.name: Oregano
crops.notes: Perennial; harvest before flowering for best flavour.

crops.name: Sage
crops.notes: Perennial. Harvest in spring/fall. Repels cabbage moth.

crops.name: Rosemary
crops.notes: Perennial shrub; drought-tolerant once established.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to zone 7 (10°F); protect in zones 5-6.

crops.name: Mint
crops.notes: Invasive — contain in pots or raised bed sections.
crops.frost_note: Perennial; rhizomes survive hard freezes, re-sprouts in spring.

crops.name: Lemon Balm
crops.notes: Perennial; lemon-scented tea herb. Self-seeds freely.
crops.frost_note: Perennial; very cold hardy, re-sprouts from roots.

crops.name: Tarragon
crops.notes: Perennial; propagate by division — true French doesn't set seed.
crops.frost_note: Perennial; dormant in winter, re-sprouts reliably.

crops.name: Fennel
crops.notes: Allelopathic — keep away from most crops.

crops.name: Chervil
crops.notes: Delicate anise flavour; bolts fast. Spring/fall only.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 25°F; preferred for cool-season production.

crops.name: Lavender
crops.notes: Perennial; valuable aromatic herb and pollinator magnet.

crops.name: Chamomile
crops.notes: Self-seeding annual. Dry flowers for premium tea market.

crops.name: Tomato
crops.notes: Trellised indeterminate. 1 row 18in spacing. Sucker weekly.

crops.name: Cherry Tomato
crops.notes: Indeterminate; incredibly productive. Cracks in wet weather.

crops.name: Pepper
crops.notes: Needs long season. Start seeds 10 wks before last frost.

crops.name: Jalapeño
crops.notes: Popular specialty pepper. High yield per plant.

crops.name: Eggplant
crops.notes: Japanese type; slender and prolific. Loves heat.

crops.name: Tomatillo
crops.notes: Plant 2+ for pollination. Dehusk and sell fresh.

crops.name: Ground Cherry
crops.notes: Unusual husk fruit; premium market item.

crops.name: Habanero
crops.notes: Very hot; specialty market. Needs long warmth.

crops.name: Cucumber
crops.notes: Trellised. 25 plants per 25ft. Interplant basil.

crops.name: Zucchini
crops.notes: Pick every 2 days or fruit overgrows.

crops.name: Patty Pan Squash
crops.notes: Novelty shape; excellent CSA box item.

crops.name: Butternut Squash
crops.notes: Winter squash; cures for 2 weeks after harvest. Storage crop.

crops.name: Kabocha Squash
crops.notes: Japanese winter squash; sweet dense flesh. Premium market.

crops.name: Delicata Squash
crops.notes: Thin skin; no peeling needed. Popular CSA item.

crops.name: Pumpkin
crops.notes: Decorative and culinary; heavy space user.

crops.name: Cantaloupe
crops.notes: Needs long warm season. High sugar at slippage.

crops.name: Watermelon
crops.notes: Needs hot long season. Very large space requirement.

crops.name: Bitter Melon
crops.notes: Specialty Asian market crop. Trellis required. High value.

crops.name: Sunflower
crops.notes: Single-stem cutting type. Succession every 10 days.

crops.name: Zinnia
crops.notes: Top cut-flower crop. Full sun. Large blooms.

crops.name: Snapdragon
crops.notes: Cool-season annual. Spring and fall planting.

crops.name: Calendula
crops.notes: Self-seeding; companion plant. Edible petals.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 25°F; self-sows. Light frost improves blooms.

crops.name: Statice
crops.notes: Everlasting; fresh and dried market. Long season.

crops.name: Strawflower
crops.notes: Everlasting; excellent dried flower market.

crops.name: Lisianthus
crops.notes: Very high value cut flower. Long lead time from seed.

crops.name: Marigold
crops.notes: Pest deterrent companion. Edible petals.

crops.name: Nasturtium
crops.notes: Edible flowers and leaves. Excellent companion plant.

crops.name: Amaranth
crops.notes: Grain and greens dual-use. Ornamental value too.

crops.name: Quinoa
crops.notes: Cool-season grain. Rinse seeds to remove saponins.

crops.name: Sweet Corn
crops.notes: Plant in blocks for pollination. Three Sisters companion.

crops.name: Asparagus
crops.notes: Perennial; 3-year establishment. Very high long-term value.

crops.name: Globe Artichoke
crops.notes: Perennial in mild climates. Annual in cold zones. Large space user.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 20°F with mulch; perennial in zones 7+.

crops.name: Celery
crops.notes: High moisture needs. Blanch stalks for white celery.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 28°F; fall crop with row cover.

crops.name: Okra
crops.notes: Heat-loving Southern crop. Harvest small for tender pods.

crops.name: Rhubarb
crops.notes: Perennial; harvest stalks only — leaves are toxic.

crops.name: Cover Crop
crops.notes: Seed 4-6 weeks before first frost. Establishes, overwinters, and is tilled under in spring (April). Full maturity by Aug following year if not tilled.

crops.name: Cover Crop
crops.notes: Fast summer cover crop. Sow in warm months; mow before seed set (35-45 days). Winter-kills on first frost.

crops.name: Cover Crop
crops.notes: Cool-season cover. Sow spring or fall. Fixes nitrogen. Till under or mow.

crops.name: Cover Crop
crops.notes: Sow late summer/fall. Winter-kills on sustained freeze, leaving weed-suppressing mulch. No spring tillage needed.

crops.name: Pole Bean
crops.notes: Classic heirloom; prolific over 60-day window.

crops.name: Pole Bean
crops.notes: Purple-streaked pods; fresh or dried.

crops.name: Dragon Tongue Bean
crops.notes: Yellow with purple streaks; harvest at 5-6".

crops.name: Yard-Long Bean
crops.notes: Harvest at 12-18"; before seeds mature.

crops.name: Lima Bean
crops.notes: Baby lima; shell or dry.

crops.name: Lima Bean
crops.notes: Large-seeded; excellent frozen.

crops.name: Black Bean
crops.notes: Dry bean; harvest when pods rattle.

crops.name: Pinto Bean
crops.notes: Classic dry bean.

crops.name: Kidney Bean
crops.notes: Large dry bean for chili.

crops.name: Cannellini Bean
crops.notes: Italian white dry bean.

crops.name: Navy Bean
crops.notes: Small white dry bean.

crops.name: Chickpea
crops.notes: Direct sow in cool spring.

crops.name: Lentil
crops.notes: Cool-season dry crop.

crops.name: Lentil
crops.notes: Premium black lentil.

crops.name: Mung Bean
crops.notes: Great for sprouts; harvest pods green or dry.

crops.name: Adzuki Bean
crops.notes: Japanese red bean; needs long season.

crops.name: Black-Eyed Pea
crops.notes: Southern staple; heat-tolerant.

crops.name: Purple Hull Pea
crops.notes: Harvest when hull turns purple.

crops.name: Haricot Vert
crops.notes: Extra-fine French filet bean; pencil-width harvest.

crops.name: Yellow Wax Bean
crops.notes: Bright yellow pods; excellent fresh market.

crops.name: Purple Wax Bean
crops.notes: Turns green when cooked; striking at market.

crops.name: Snow Pea
crops.notes: Large flat edible pods.

crops.name: Pigeon Pea
crops.notes: Tropical; green or dry use.

crops.name: Tepary Bean
crops.notes: Drought-tolerant Southwestern bean.

crops.name: Scarlet Runner
crops.notes: Ornamental and edible; red flowers.

crops.name: Hyacinth Bean
crops.notes: Ornamental purple pods; used dried.

crops.name: Fingerling Potato
crops.notes: Hill twice during season.

crops.name: Purple Potato
crops.notes: Deep purple flesh; anthocyanin-rich.

crops.name: Russet Potato
crops.notes: Classic baking potato.

crops.name: Yellow Potato
crops.notes: Buttery yellow flesh; excellent roasting.

crops.name: Jicama
crops.notes: Mexican turnip; needs long season.

crops.name: Taro / Dasheen
crops.notes: Tropical root; corms edible.

crops.name: Horseradish
crops.notes: Perennial; harvest roots in fall.

crops.name: Burdock
crops.notes: Needs deep well-worked soil to 3 ft.

crops.name: Scorzonera
crops.notes: Black-skinned; sweet oyster flavor.

crops.name: Watermelon Radish
crops.notes: Stunning pink interior.

crops.name: Purple Daikon
crops.notes: Purple outside; white flesh.

crops.name: Black Radish
crops.notes: Pungent; great for storage.

crops.name: Carrot
crops.notes: Purple outside; orange inside.

crops.name: Carrot
crops.notes: Short thick roots for heavy soils.

crops.name: Carrot
crops.notes: Creamy white; mild sweet flavor.

crops.name: Golden Beet
crops.notes: Non-bleeding; buttery flavor.

crops.name: Purple Top Turnip
crops.notes: Classic storage; roots and greens.

crops.name: Oca
crops.notes: Andean tuber; harvest after frost.

crops.name: Lettuce
crops.notes: Soft buttery heads; excellent flavor.

crops.name: Lettuce
crops.notes: Small tender heads; gourmet appeal.

crops.name: Lettuce
crops.notes: Lacy lobed leaves; slow to bolt.

crops.name: Lettuce
crops.notes: Deep red lobed leaves; striking in mix.

crops.name: Lettuce
crops.notes: Red romaine; beautiful at market.
crops.frost_note: More frost hardy than green romaine.

crops.name: Lettuce
crops.notes: Mini romaine; crisp and sweet.

crops.name: Lettuce
crops.notes: Deeply frilled red leaves; beautiful.

crops.name: Lettuce
crops.notes: Austrian heirloom; red-speckled romaine.

crops.name: Lettuce
crops.notes: Classic crisphead; needs cooler temps to form.

crops.name: Lettuce
crops.notes: Tongue-shaped leaves; Amish heirloom; slow to bolt.

crops.name: Amaranth Greens
crops.notes: Heat-tolerant; leaves and stems edible; cut-and-come.

crops.name: Orach
crops.notes: Drought-tolerant spinach substitute; bolt-resistant.

crops.name: New Zealand Spinach
crops.notes: Heat-tolerant spinach substitute; cut-and-come.

crops.name: Malabar Spinach
crops.notes: Tropical vine; excellent summer green.

crops.name: Claytonia
crops.notes: Mild winter salad green; self-seeds freely.

crops.name: Dandelion
crops.notes: Perennial; harvest leaves before flowering.

crops.name: Chicory
crops.notes: Italian specialty; harvest shoots in winter.

crops.name: Pac Choi
crops.notes: Uniform full-size bok choy.

crops.name: Yu Choy
crops.notes: Fast Chinese green; harvest before flowering.

crops.name: Gai Lan
crops.notes: Harvest florets and stems; repeated cuts.

crops.name: Komatsuna
crops.notes: Japanese spinach-mustard hybrid; extremely fast.

crops.name: Perpetual Spinach
crops.notes: Chard-like; slow to bolt; long harvest window.

crops.name: Tomato
crops.notes: Classic paste/sauce tomato; determinate; meaty flesh.

crops.name: Tomato
crops.notes: Italian DOP heirloom paste tomato.

crops.name: Heirloom Tomato
crops.notes: Dusky purple-red; rich complex flavor.

crops.name: Heirloom Tomato
crops.notes: Golden heirloom; low-acid sweet flavor.

crops.name: Heirloom Tomato
crops.notes: Lime-green with yellow stripes; tart flavor.

crops.name: Heirloom Tomato
crops.notes: Dark mahogany; rich smoky flavor; Russian heirloom.

crops.name: Heirloom Tomato
crops.notes: Up to 2 lb fruits; prolific producer.

crops.name: Tomato
crops.notes: Disease-resistant slicing tomato; very reliable.

crops.name: Tomato
crops.notes: First slicing tomato of the season.

crops.name: Cherry Tomato
crops.notes: Pear-shaped; prolific; sweet and mild.

crops.name: Cherry Tomato
crops.notes: Deep purple-red; complex rich flavor.

crops.name: Grape Tomato
crops.notes: Crack-resistant grape; prolific; great flavor.

crops.name: Cherry Tomato
crops.notes: Classic red cherry; reliable and prolific.

crops.name: Tomato
crops.notes: Large oxheart paste tomato; meaty.

crops.name: Pepper
crops.notes: Mild; 6-8" pods; green or red; great for roasting.

crops.name: Pepper
crops.notes: Heart-shaped; harvest green or red-dried as ancho.

crops.name: Pepper
crops.notes: Hot; thin-walled; great for salsa.

crops.name: Banana Pepper
crops.notes: Sweet; pale yellow to red; excellent pickled.

crops.name: Shishito Pepper
crops.notes: Japanese; blister/char whole; 1 in 10 spicy.

crops.name: Padrón Pepper
crops.notes: Spanish tapas pepper; harvest small and green.

crops.name: Cayenne Pepper
crops.notes: Long slim; harvest red; dry or fresh.

crops.name: Ghost Pepper
crops.notes: Extremely hot; needs long warm season.

crops.name: Fresno Pepper
crops.notes: Similar to jalapeño but hotter when red.

crops.name: Cubanelle Pepper
crops.notes: Italian frying pepper; thin walls; mild sweet.

crops.name: Mini Sweet Pepper
crops.notes: Snacking pepper; red/orange/yellow; sweet.

crops.name: Aji Amarillo
crops.notes: Peruvian; fruity heat; essential in Peruvian cuisine.

crops.name: Bell Pepper
crops.notes: Chocolate-brown bell; sweet; striking at market.

crops.name: Pepperoncini
crops.notes: Italian / Greek pickled pepper; mild.

crops.name: Thai Bird Pepper
crops.notes: Very hot; tiny pods; prolific; dries well.

crops.name: Acorn Squash
crops.notes: Small ribbed; 1-2 lb; harvest when skin darkens.

crops.name: Red Kuri Squash
crops.notes: Pear-shaped; chestnut flavor; keeps well.

crops.name: Sugar Pie Pumpkin
crops.notes: Classic 5-8 lb pie pumpkin; sweet dense flesh.

crops.name: Cinderella Pumpkin
crops.notes: Flat French heirloom; 20-35 lbs; stunning at market.

crops.name: Blue Hubbard
crops.notes: Excellent keeper to 6 months; superb flavor.

crops.name: Spaghetti Squash
crops.notes: Strands like pasta when cooked; popular specialty.

crops.name: Sweet Dumpling Squash
crops.notes: Small striped; single-serving size; sweet.

crops.name: Delicata Squash
crops.notes: Bush habit; cream with green stripes; edible skin.

crops.name: Butternut Squash
crops.notes: Early butternut; smooth tan skin; excellent flavor.

crops.name: Kabocha Squash
crops.notes: Early maturing kabocha; dry dense flesh.

crops.name: Round Zucchini
crops.notes: Baseball-size; harvest at golf ball; stuff and bake.

crops.name: Yellow Crookneck
crops.notes: Classic Southern crookneck; harvest small.

crops.name: Tromboncino
crops.notes: Trombone-shaped Italian; harvest young or winter squash.

crops.name: Lemon Squash
crops.notes: Round yellow; harvest at lemon-size; nutty flavor.

crops.name: Cousa Squash
crops.notes: Lebanese; light green; harvest at 4-6"; creamy flavor.

crops.name: Lemon Cucumber
crops.notes: Round yellow cucumber; mild sweet flavor.

crops.name: Armenian Cucumber
crops.notes: Long pale-green; very mild; slice at 12-18".

crops.name: English Cucumber
crops.notes: Seedless; thin-skinned; no bitterness.

crops.name: Persian Cucumber
crops.notes: 5-6" mini cucumbers; crisp; snack-size.

crops.name: Pickling Cucumber
crops.notes: Short blocky; classic pickling cucumber.

crops.name: Japanese Cucumber
crops.notes: Long ribbed; excellent thin skin; slice or pickle.

crops.name: Crystal Apple Cucumber
crops.notes: Round white cucumber; very mild; heritage variety.

crops.name: Honeydew Melon
crops.notes: Smooth white-green skin; creamy green flesh.

crops.name: Orange-Flesh Honeydew
crops.notes: Jade skin; bright orange flesh; tropical flavor.

crops.name: Galia Melon
crops.notes: Israeli; aromatic and sweet; net-skinned cantaloupe type.

crops.name: Canary Melon
crops.notes: Bright yellow oblong; spicy sweet white flesh.

crops.name: Charentais Cantaloupe
crops.notes: French heirloom; very short shelf life; supreme flavor.

crops.name: Cantaloupe
crops.notes: Classic netted cantaloupe; reliable producer.

crops.name: Watermelon
crops.notes: Heirloom; yellow spots; 15-40 lb fruits.

crops.name: Watermelon
crops.notes: Small yellow-flesh watermelon; 6-8 lbs; early.

crops.name: Crenshaw Melon
crops.notes: Large 8-10 lb; spicy-sweet peachy flesh.

crops.name: Thai Basil
crops.notes: Anise-clove flavor; purple flowers; heat tolerant.

crops.name: Purple Basil
crops.notes: Striking purple; milder flavor; great as garnish.

crops.name: Lemon Basil
crops.notes: Intense lemon fragrance; culinary and cocktail herb.

crops.name: Cilantro
crops.notes: Slower to bolt than standard; succession every 3 weeks.
crops.frost_note: Frost tolerant; best in cool weather.

crops.name: Vietnamese Coriander
crops.notes: Strong coriander flavor; tropical perennial; no bolting.

crops.name: Culantro
crops.notes: Spiny-leafed; stronger than cilantro; Caribbean essential.

crops.name: Peppermint
crops.notes: Perennial; very vigorous; contain with barriers.

crops.name: Apple Mint
crops.notes: Mild fruity mint; excellent in beverages.

crops.name: Korean Mint
crops.notes: Anise-licorice; perennial in zones 5+; pollinator magnet.

crops.name: Lemon Verbena
crops.notes: Intensely lemon; use fresh or dry; perennial in warm zones.
crops.frost_note: Frost sensitive; container in cold climates.

crops.name: Lemon Thyme
crops.notes: Golden variegated; strong lemon fragrance.

crops.name: Caraway
crops.notes: Biennial; harvest seeds in second year; rye companion.

crops.name: Fenugreek
crops.notes: Harvest leaves young; seeds dry for spice.

crops.name: Marjoram
crops.notes: Sweeter than oregano; tender perennial grown as annual.

crops.name: Summer Savory
crops.notes: Annual; pairs with beans; peppery flavor.

crops.name: Winter Savory
crops.notes: Perennial; stronger flavor than summer; woody plant.

crops.name: Borage
crops.notes: Blue edible flowers; cucumber flavor; self-seeds freely.

crops.name: Lovage
crops.notes: Perennial; celery-like; powerful flavor; food forest plant.

crops.name: Stevia
crops.notes: Natural sweetener; harvest before flowering; 300x sweeter.

crops.name: Epazote
crops.notes: Mexican cooking herb; essential with black beans.

crops.name: Echinacea
crops.notes: Medicinal perennial; harvest roots in year 3+.

crops.name: Ashwagandha
crops.notes: Adaptogen root; harvest in fall of first year.

crops.name: Dahlia
crops.notes: Tubers; stake at planting; pinch at 3 ft for branching.
crops.frost_note: Frost sensitive; lift tubers before frost.

crops.name: Ranunculus
crops.notes: Corm; plant in fall for spring; sensational cut flower.

crops.name: Anemone
crops.notes: Corm-like rhizome; fall or spring plant; bright poppy-like blooms.

crops.name: Sweet Pea
crops.notes: Direct sow in fall or early spring; needs cool temps.
crops.frost_note: Hardy to 25°F; needs cool to set buds.

crops.name: Delphinium
crops.notes: Spike flowers; stately; stake in wind; excellent for formal arrangements.

crops.name: Larkspur
crops.notes: Direct sow in fall or early spring; self-seeds; beautiful spikes.
crops.frost_note: Very frost tolerant; fall sow for best results.

crops.name: Foxglove
crops.notes: Biennial; plant in spring; blooms second year; stunning spikes.

crops.name: Cosmos
crops.notes: Easy direct sow; self-seeds; succession sow for cut flowers.

crops.name: Cosmos
crops.notes: Hot orange/yellow tones; heat-tolerant; excellent wildlife value.

crops.name: Mexican Sunflower
crops.notes: Brilliant orange; 3-4 ft tall; excellent for arrangements.

crops.name: Bachelor's Button
crops.notes: Direct sow early; great filler; dries well.
crops.frost_note: Very frost tolerant; fall or early spring sow.

crops.name: Scabiosa
crops.notes: Pincushion flowers; lavender-blue; excellent pollinator.

crops.name: Rudbeckia
crops.notes: Brilliant yellow with dark eye; long stem form; excellent cut.
crops.frost_note: Frost sensitive as annual; perennial in zones 4+.

crops.name: Celosia
crops.notes: Crested form; brilliant red/gold/pink; dries beautifully.

crops.name: Celosia
crops.notes: Feathery plumes; very long-lasting cut flower.

crops.name: Globe Amaranth
crops.notes: Clover-like globes; dries perfectly; heat-tolerant.

crops.name: Liatris
crops.notes: Tall purple spikes; blooms top-down; exceptional cut flower.

crops.name: Statice
crops.notes: Papery; dries perfectly; mixed arrangement filler.

crops.name: Hard Red Wheat
crops.notes: Winter wheat; plant in fall; harvest July; 60-80 bu/acre.

crops.name: Spelt
crops.notes: Ancient grain; nutty flavor; hulled; winter sow.

crops.name: Einkorn
crops.notes: Oldest cultivated wheat; excellent nutrition; hull before milling.

crops.name: Hulless Barley
crops.notes: Spring sow; threshes free of hull; great for home milling.

crops.name: Oats
crops.notes: Ready to eat without processing; spring sow.

crops.name: Flint Corn
crops.notes: Stunning rainbow colors; ornamental and edible; dry corn.

crops.name: Field Corn
crops.notes: Deep red heirloom field corn; excellent dried masa.

crops.name: Popcorn
crops.notes: Harvest at full dry; excellent pops.

crops.name: Sweet Sorghum
crops.notes: Make syrup from stalks; grain edible; drought tolerant.

crops.name: Pearl Millet
crops.notes: Heat and drought tolerant; edible grain.

crops.name: Teff
crops.notes: Ethiopian staple grain; tiny seeds; gluten-free.

crops.name: Buckwheat
crops.notes: Fast summer crop; white flowers; edible grain; gluten-free.
crops.frost_note: Frost sensitive; also excellent cover crop.

crops.name: Strawberry
crops.notes: Day-neutral; fruits spring through fall; matted row system.

crops.name: Alpine Strawberry
crops.notes: Small intense berries all season; no runners.

crops.name: Raspberry
crops.notes: Cut canes to ground in fall; bears on new canes.

crops.name: Thornless Blackberry
crops.notes: Semi-erect; trellis; heavy producer.

crops.name: Elderberry
crops.notes: Medicinal; flowers and berries edible; pollinator plant.

crops.name: Honeyberry / Haskap
crops.notes: Very early berry; first fruit of spring.

crops.name: Red Currant
crops.notes: Sour; jam and jelly; tolerates part shade.

crops.name: Black Currant
crops.notes: Very high vitamin C; jam and juice.

crops.name: Aronia
crops.notes: Extremely high antioxidants; astringent fresh; excellent processed.

crops.name: Goji Berry
crops.notes: Dry red berries; superfood status; drought-tolerant once established.

crops.name: Cover Crop
crops.notes: Fast summer biomass; chop and incorporate; smothers weeds.

crops.name: Cover Crop
crops.notes: Fast tropical legume; 100+ lbs N/acre; nematode suppressor.

crops.name: Cover Crop
crops.notes: Bio-drills 18"+ deep; winter-kills releasing nitrogen.

crops.name: Cover Crop
crops.notes: Biofumigant; suppresses soil pathogens; fast biomass.

crops.name: Cover Crop
crops.notes: Outstanding pollinator cover; lavender flowers; winter kills.

crops.name: Cover Crop
crops.notes: Fast nitrogen fixer; can mow multiple times.

crops.name: Cover Crop
crops.notes: Winter cover; fixes nitrogen; very cold-hardy.

crops.name: Cover Crop
crops.notes: Quick cover; edible tops and roots; livestock forage.

crops.name: Cover Crop
crops.notes: Excellent weed suppression; wildlife habitat; warm season.

crops.name: Cover Crop
crops.notes: Flood tolerant; excellent reseeder; spring cover.

crops.name: Globe Artichoke
crops.notes: Purple Italian; tender and nutty; perennial in zones 7+.

crops.name: Asparagus
crops.notes: Sweeter than green; harvest year 3+.

crops.name: Asparagus
crops.notes: Classic green; all-male hybrid; productive.

crops.name: Thai Eggplant
crops.notes: Golf ball size; used whole in Thai curries.

crops.name: White Eggplant
crops.notes: White skin; mild creamy flavor; no bitterness.

crops.name: Luffa Gourd
crops.notes: Edible young; sponge when dry; 6-month season.
crops.frost_note: Frost sensitive; needs very long season.

crops.name: Ginger
crops.notes: Plant rhizomes in spring; harvest at 8-10 months.

crops.name: Turmeric
crops.notes: Bright orange rhizomes; harvest after tops die back.

crops.name: Lemongrass
crops.notes: Tropical grass; perennial in zone 9+; harvest stalks.

crops.name: Okra
crops.notes: Striking red pods; harvest at 3-4"; stays tender.

crops.name: Cutting Celery
crops.notes: Leaf celery; easier to grow than stalk; intense flavor.

crops.name: Kohlrabi
crops.notes: Fast; harvest at golf ball; crisp and sweet.

crops.name: Sweet Corn
crops.notes: Classic white sweet corn; excellent eating quality.
